draft_hit_rate_z.what: Percentage of draft picks who started 2+ NFL seasons
draft_hit_rate_z.how: Binary hit (2+ seasons started) / total picks. Excludes last 2 years (development time).
draft_hit_rate_z.limits: Treats all starters equally — a replacement-level starter counts the same as a Pro Bowler. See Draft Quality for a richer signal.

draft_quality_score_z.what: Average quality tier per draft pick — rewards Pro Bowlers and All-Pros, not just starters
draft_quality_score_z.how: Each pick scored: Bust=0, Contributor (2-3 seasons started)=1, Solid starter (4+)=2, Pro Bowler=3, All-Pro=5. Average across all mature picks.
draft_quality_score_z.limits: Pro Bowls and All-Pros take years to accumulate. Recent GMs are disadvantaged — their picks haven't had time to earn accolades yet.

day3_gem_rate_z.what: Average quality tier for Day 3 picks (rounds 4-7) — finding Amon-Ra St. Brown in the 4th is worth more than finding a camp body
day3_gem_rate_z.how: Same tiered scoring as Draft Quality but restricted to rounds 4-7. An All-Pro Day 3 pick scores 5; a bust scores 0.
day3_gem_rate_z.limits: Small sample per GM (typically 3-5 Day 3 picks per year). A single All-Pro find can dominate.

first_round_success_z.what: How much value each first-round pick produces per season vs what's expected at that draft slot
first_round_success_z.how: Actual weighted AV per season played minus the historical expected AV/season for that pick number. Reduces recency bias by measuring rate rather than volume.
first_round_success_z.limits: AV isn't a perfect value metric. Players in their first 1-2 seasons may not yet reflect their true production rate.

fa_hit_rate_z.what: Percentage of all FA signings who played 12+ games in year 1
fa_hit_rate_z.how: Includes every signing where the player came from a different team — camp bodies, waiver claims, and splashy deals. Hit = 12+ games in signing season.
fa_hit_rate_z.limits: Counts all signings equally. A $15M/yr starter counts the same as a vet-min depth piece.

fa_value_efficiency_z.what: FA hits per $10M of APY spent
fa_value_efficiency_z.how: Total FA hits / (total APY in $M / 10). Rewards getting production without overspending.
fa_value_efficiency_z.limits: Treats all hits equally regardless of contract size. Doesn't capture surplus value on individual signings.

trade_surplus_z.what: Draft pick value received minus given via trade (Jimmy Johnson chart)
trade_surplus_z.how: Sum of pick values on pieces received minus sum of pick values given across all trades in tenure.
trade_surplus_z.limits: JJ chart overvalues top picks. Player-for-picks trades (where a team gives up a player for draft capital) aren't captured by pick value alone.

trade_activity_z.what: Distinct trades per season as GM
trade_activity_z.how: Count of unique trades divided by seasons as GM.
trade_activity_z.limits: Pure volume. High activity can reflect skill or chaos. Pair with trade surplus.

core_retention_z.what: Percentage of own draft picks re-signed to second contract
core_retention_z.how: Of picks made in GM's tenure through 2020 (enough time for rookie deals to expire), what % got re-signed by this same GM.
core_retention_z.limits: Requires 4-5 years of tenure to be meaningful. NaN for newer GMs. Penalizes GMs who correctly let busts walk.

udfa_hit_rate_z.what: UDFAs who stuck on the roster 2+ years
udfa_hit_rate_z.how: UDFAs = contract holders NOT in draft picks table. Hit = played in signing year AND year+1.
udfa_hit_rate_z.limits: Small sample for most GMs. One or two hits swing the rate dramatically.
